---
package: "openpipeline_qc"
version: "v0.3.0"
title: "generate_html"
namespace: "Ingestion qc"
description: "Generate an HTML report from the QC metrics"
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline_qc v0.3.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `generate_html`  
Namespace: `ingestion_qc`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_qc/tree/v0.3.0/src/ingestion_qc/generate_html)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_qc)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_qc/v0.3.0/components/ingestion_qc/generate_html)

:::




## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name                |Description                                              |Attributes                                       |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input_data`      |The input JSON file containing the QC metrics            |`file`, required, example: `"path/to/file.json"` |
|`--input_structure` |The input JSON file containing the structure of the data |`file`, required, example: `"path/to/file.json"` |


### Outputs

|Name                 |Description            |Attributes                                       |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output_qc_report` |The output HTML report |`file`, required, example: `"path/to/file.html"` |

## Authors


  * Jakub Majercik [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/jakubmajercik) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/jakubmajercik) (author)

  * Dorien Roosen [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/dorien-er) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dorien-roosen) (author)

  * Robrecht Cannoodt [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/rcannood) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0003-3641-729X) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/robrechtcannoodt) (author)

  * Weiwei Schultz (contributor)

## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_qc \
  -r v0.3.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/ingestion_qc/generate_html/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_qc/v0.3.0/components/ingestion_qc/generate_html).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_qc \
  -r v0.3.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/ingestion_qc/generate_html/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).
